In this position, White has a strong initiative with a central pawn majority (d4, e5), active pieces (knights on c3 and e4, bishop on c1), and a potential attack on Black's king (e.g., via the e-file or f3-f5 push). Black's pieces are slightly passive, and White's last move (e2-d3) reinforces control over the center, giving White a clear advantage.
